title: Wiggle Theory
definition: The theory that everything in the universe is composed of vibrating strings or wiggles.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Imagine the smallest particles as tiny, vibrating strings. Everything, including you and me, is made up of these wiggly strings.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: String theory proposes that all matter in the universe is composed of one-dimensional, vibrating objects called strings. The properties and interactions of these strings define the behavior of particles at every scale.
difficulty_levels.expert: Wiggle Theory suggests a unified framework for understanding reality, from the quantum level to the macroscopic scale, by treating fundamental constituents as vibrational modes of one-dimensional objects. This approach dissolves the boundary between object and process, fundamentally altering our understanding of the nature of physical phenomena.

extra_fields.insight: The universe is not just made of things; it's an interconnected network of vibrations or wiggles.
